title: SSL Handshake Failure with HTTP Request Node
symptom: When using the HTTP Request node to connect to a specific HTTPS site, an SSL handshake failure occurs. The error message indicates a failure in the SSL routines.
not_this_if: The error does not occur with other URLs
root_cause: The SSL handshake failure typically occurs when the server's SSL certificate is invalid, expired, or when the server does not support HTTPS connections. In this case, the specific domain does not support HTTPS, leading to the handshake failure.
fix_steps: Change the URL from HTTPS to HTTP for the affected domain
Test the connection again using the HTTP Request node
agent_instruction: Use the HTTP version of the URL instead of the HTTPS version for the request.
